question.content: Partition the 6x8 grid into connected regions so that each region contains exactly one number and its area equals that number. Output exactly one token SEE_STEP4, plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no sentence, and no extra text.

step_1.content: Grow regions from constrained clues first and keep each region connected during expansion.
step_1.key_point: Extract the key constraints before committing to the final choice.

step_3.content: Apply area bounds to stop overgrowth and force neighboring boundaries.
step_3.key_point: Each region must keep exactly one clue cell.

step_5.content: Use connectivity checks to resolve remaining ambiguous cells.
step_5.key_point: Disconnected fragments are invalid and must be merged or rejected.

step_7.content: Finalize all region borders so every clue-matching area is satisfied.
step_7.key_point: The completed partition is shown in the last step figure.
